rules.rule: Define authority as a strict precedence chain with explicit layers, not a flat list of superiors.
rules.do: List operative authority (COO controller, runtime contract) as the binding constraint. List static documents under a separate 'Reference evidence' heading. Example: 'Reports to: COO controller for the current turn. Reference evidence: docs/VISION.md, docs/v0/architecture.md (not competing runtime authority).'
rules.dont: Put governance documents in 'subordinate_to' as if they were a reporting chain. Example of violation: 'Subordinate to: docs/VISION.md, docs/architecture.md' ג€” this conflates reference material with operative authority.
rules.source: Runs 003-006: flagged in every run. Reviewers consistently rejected flat subordinate_to lists.

rules.rule: Use one canonical definition for every threshold or semantic concept. Define it once, reference it everywhere.
rules.do: Define 'material pushback' once in <guardrails>: 'Material pushback means any unresolved finding with blocking or major severity.' Then reference that definition: 'Freeze only when no material pushback remains (see guardrail definition).'
rules.dont: Use different phrasings for the same concept across sections. Example of violation: <role> says 'freezes only when no material pushback remains', <guardrails> says 'no unresolved blocking or major issue remains', <completion> says 'all material issues resolved' ג€” three phrasings, never reconciled.
rules.source: Runs 003-006: inconsistent freeze threshold wording flagged in every run.

rules.rule: Separate canonical artifacts (promoted on freeze) from run-scoped evidence artifacts (always written). Use explicit labels and path conventions for each.
rules.do: In <outputs>, group artifacts under 'Canonical artifacts (promoted on freeze):' and 'Run-scoped evidence (always written):' with full tool-relative paths. Example: 'Canonical: tools/agent-role-builder/role/agent-role-builder-role.md (promoted on freeze)'. 'Evidence: runs/<job-id>/rounds/round-<n>.json (always written).'
rules.dont: Mix canonical and evidence artifacts in a single list with ambiguous paths. Example of violation: listing 'agent-role-builder-role.md' without specifying whether it means the canonical promoted copy or the run-scoped draft.
rules.source: Runs 003-006: artifact lifecycle confusion flagged in every run.

rules.rule: Define mutually exclusive triggers for every terminal state. Each trigger must be a testable predicate against run evidence.
rules.do: Define each state explicitly: 'frozen: all reviewers approved or conditional AND leader confirms no material pushback. pushback: any reviewer rejected with blocking/major findings AND review budget remains. blocked: non-recoverable validation or structural error. resume_required: review budget exhausted with unresolved blocking/major findings.'
rules.dont: List terminal states without defining what triggers each one. Example of violation: <completion> says 'Terminal status reached (frozen, pushback, blocked, or resume_required)' without specifying when each applies.
rules.source: Runs 004-006: 'what exactly causes resume_required vs pushback?' asked in multiple rounds.

rules.rule: The self-check must validate what it claims to validate. If it reports 'passed', every claimed check must actually execute. Use semantic matching for concept coverage, not literal string matching.
rules.do: Check out-of-scope coverage by extracting keywords from each out_of_scope item and verifying the <scope> section contains those concepts. Report which keywords were checked and which matched. Example: 'Out-of-scope: Tool creation ג€” checked keywords: tool, creation ג€” found in <scope>: yes.'
rules.dont: Use markdown.includes(item) for out-of-scope verification. Example of violation: contract says 'Tool creation (owned by llm-tool-builder)', markdown says 'Tool creation. That belongs to llm-tool-builder.' ג€” literal match fails, but the concept is covered.
rules.source: Runs 003-006: self-check reported 'passed' but reviewers found issues it missed. MISSING_OUT_OF_SCOPE false positives flagged repeatedly.

rules.rule: Deduplicate scope exclusions before generating the markdown. Contract out_of_scope strings and markdown <scope> not-in-scope items must align exactly.
rules.do: Merge request.out_of_scope and role_requirements.scope.not_in_scope into one deduplicated list before generating. Normalize casing and punctuation. The <scope> section should have exactly the same exclusions as the contract, no more, no fewer.
rules.dont: Append both lists without deduplication. Example of violation: not-in-scope lists 'Tool creation (owned by llm-tool-builder)' from the request AND 'Tool creation' from role_requirements ג€” same concept, two entries with different wording.
rules.source: Runs 003-006: duplicate and contradictory scope entries flagged in every run.

rules.rule: Arbitration must have a mechanical definition: trigger condition, who arbitrates, what evidence is produced, and how it affects terminal outcomes.
rules.do: Define: 'Arbitration triggers when split verdicts persist for 2+ consecutive rounds after revision. The leader arbitrates. Evidence: arbitration_used=true, arbitration_rationale in result.json. Arbitration can only resolve minor/conditional disagreements, never blocking/major items.'
rules.dont: Leave arbitration as a vague concept. Example of violation: 'If split verdicts persist and arbitration is still available, use at most one arbitration round' ג€” no defined trigger threshold, no actor, no evidence format.
rules.source: Runs 003-006: arbitration semantics flagged in every run. 'What counts as materially mixed?' had no answer.

rules.rule: Every required output declared in the contract must appear with its full tool-relative canonical path in the <outputs> section. No basename-only references.
rules.do: Use full paths: 'tools/agent-role-builder/role/agent-role-builder-role.md'. Match exactly between contract package_files and <outputs> canonical list.
rules.dont: Use basenames or slug templates. Example of violation: <outputs> says '<slug>-role.md', contract says 'agent-role-builder-role.md', promotion logic uses 'tools/agent-role-builder/role/agent-role-builder-role.md' ג€” three different representations of the same file.
rules.source: Runs 004-006: package_files path ambiguity flagged repeatedly.

rules.rule: Completion criteria must be testable against every terminal state, not just frozen. Define what 'complete' means for each state separately.
rules.do: List completion per state: 'Frozen: all canonical artifacts promoted + result.json + decision log updated. Pushback: result.json + pushback.json + decision log + board summary written to run dir. Blocked: result.json with blocking reason. Resume_required: result.json + resume-package.json with deferred items.'
rules.dont: Write completion criteria that only describe the frozen path. Example of violation: 'This workflow is complete when terminal status reached and all applicable artifacts written' ג€” does not specify which artifacts are applicable for pushback vs blocked.
rules.source: Runs 003-006: completion section only described frozen. Reviewers asked 'is a pushback run complete?' in multiple rounds.

rules.rule: Context-gathering is a precondition phase executed before Step 1. It is not part of the numbered execution steps.
rules.do: Label context-gathering as 'Preconditions (before Step 1):' and number steps starting from 1 for the first execution action. Example: '<context-gathering> 1. Load request JSON (precondition). 2. Verify source_refs (precondition).' Then '<steps> Step 1: Validate and normalize.'
rules.dont: Mix context-gathering items into the numbered step flow. Example of violation: context-gathering lists 6 items, Step 1 starts at '1. Validate' ג€” is validation step 1 or step 7? Ambiguous.
rules.source: Run 003: reviewer asked whether context-gathering is step 0 or part of step 1.

rules.rule: When a reviewer finding is carried forward unresolved across rounds, the next round must explicitly address it by ID. Silent carry-forward blocks convergence.
rules.do: Track each finding by its conceptual_group ID across rounds. When revising, reference the specific group: 'Addressing group-3 from round 1: added explicit lifecycle per terminal state.' In the fix items map, list every carried-forward finding as either accepted (with fix summary) or rejected (with reason).
rules.dont: Let unresolved findings accumulate without tracking. Example of violation: round 0 flags 'mixed verdict not resolved', round 1 flags 'mixed verdict not resolved' with different wording, round 2 flags it again ג€” same issue, three descriptions, no explicit closure chain.
rules.source: Runs 004-006: mixed reviewer verdicts from prior rounds were not tracked as specific items to resolve.

rules.rule: Board roster validation must require at least one complete Codex+Claude reviewer pair at request validation time. This is a hard gate, not an inference.
rules.do: In validator.ts: check reviewer_count >= 2, check reviewers array length matches reviewer_count, check every pair contains one codex + one claude provider. Fail validation if any check fails. In <inputs>: state 'At request validation time, reviewer_count must be 2, 4, or 6, and the roster must contain at least one complete live Codex+Claude reviewer pair.'
rules.dont: Rely on the roster schema alone without explicit pair validation. Example of violation: reviewer_count=2 with two Codex reviewers ג€” schema allows it but governance requires cross-provider pairs.
rules.source: Run 004: reviewer flagged that pair requirement was implied but not enforced in validation.

rules.rule: Decision history must be preserved across update and fix operations. Define the exact mechanism: append to existing log, version with timestamp, or archive-and-replace.
rules.do: State explicitly: 'On update/fix operations, the existing decision-log.md is appended with a new dated section. Prior entries are never deleted. The board-summary.md is replaced with the current run summary but the prior version is retained in the run directory.'
rules.dont: Leave preservation mechanism undefined. Example of violation: guardrail says 'Decision history must be preserved across update/fix operations' but Step 5 says 'Write decision log' ג€” does 'write' mean overwrite or append?
rules.source: Runs 003-004: 'how is prior frozen decision history preserved on update?' had no answer.

rules.rule: The role definition must explicitly state whether each step describes current implementation behavior or target-state behavior. Mixed modes without labels create false expectations.
rules.do: If a step describes implemented behavior: 'Step 3: Self-check (current implementation). The self-check currently validates XML tag presence and semantic out-of-scope keyword matching.' If target-state: 'Step 3: Self-check (target). The self-check will validate structural coherence and lifecycle consistency (not yet implemented).'
rules.dont: Describe target-state behavior as if it's implemented. Example of violation: Step 3 says 'self-check validates structural coherence and lifecycle consistency' but the actual validator.ts only checks tag presence ג€” the reviewer tests the claim and finds it false.
rules.source: Run 003: reviewer asked 'does Step 3 describe what the self-check actually does or what it should do?' Answer was ambiguous.

rules.rule: Every conditional guard in the execution loop must be verified against all terminal states. Artifacts that should be produced on every round must not be gated by revision-specific conditions.
applies_to: any conditional guard on artifact writing
rules.do: For every guard that controls artifact writing: list all states (frozen, pushback, blocked, resume_required, final round, no unresolved). Verify the guard allows the artifact in each applicable state. Example: learning engine runs on every round (learning is valuable even on the final round), revision only runs when canRevise=true.
rules.dont: Gate a universal artifact behind a revision-specific condition. Example:  gates learning.json and diff-summary.json — but learning should capture rules even on the final round.
rules.source: Run 008 + board rewrite review NF-1: learning engine and diff-summary skipped on final round due to overly restrictive guard.
